title: T4 lysozyme L99A cavity mutant (3DMV) — 50 ns apo cavity characterization

C1.note: 3DMV fetched/cleaned; L99A confirmed (PLAN.md: residue 99 = ALA). Crystallization adducts BME/HED/PO4/CL stripped with explicit distance justification (12.0/14.8/26.8 Aa from cavity centroid). propka3 run (HIS31->HID, pKa 7.32). tLEaP succeeded: PROCESS_REPORT job 37880 Errors=0, NATOM=48280, 11581 OPC waters, 8 Cl- neutralizing net +8; 12 Aa pad. No residues dropped (1-164 intact).

C2.note: Every parameter tier-justified in PLAN.md FF table: ff19SB (Amber24 p.33-35, Tier2), OPC (p.34), Li-Merz 12-6 OPC ions (p.54), 12 Aa pad (cut+buffer, p.461), 298 K (X-ray/NMR ref T), 50 ns (user prompt). Manual-validation clause confirms leaprc.protein.ff19SB / leaprc.water.opc exist. No banned hardcoding phrases. Literature review with 5 PMIDs (Step 2b/2c table); PLAN approval gate present (APPROVED 2026-06-08).

C3.note: min1/min2/heat/burst/equil2/prod all PASS (PROCESS_REPORT jobs 37881-37886). prod.mdout: NSTEP=25,000,000 reached (TIME 50900 ps incl. equil offset = 50 ns prod), grep NaN = 0, AVERAGES density 1.0199 g/cc, T=298.03 K, Etot -125404 +/- 210. Backbone RMSD plateau 0.87 Aa, drift 0.0075 Aa. No blow-up.

C4.note: All three prompt observables delivered. Cavity volume: calibrated grid-probe time series (cavity_volume.dat, 500 frames, verified mean 197.2 +/- 19.3 Aa3). Water occupancy: cavity_water.dat (verified mean 0.148, 85.2% empty) + 3D water density grid water_density_cavity.dx (volmap job 37919). Transient openings: f114_gate.dat / cavity_mouth.dat, volume-gate correlation +0.20, plus RMSF/RMSD/Rg controls. Correct cpptraj + MDAnalysis methods for the question.

C5.note: Paper-confirmed (Eriksson et al. 1992, PMID 1553543, Science 255:178-183, the canonical L99A cavity reference): L99A cavity = 150 Aa3 and 'No solvent molecules were observed in any of these cavities' (dry apolar cavity). Liu/Quillin/Matthews 2008 (PMID 18780783) confirms nonpolar T4L cavities are dry/diffuse. Agent matches hydration well: 85% dry, 0-1 transient water (vs key '0-2 waters'). Cavity volume MD mean 197 Aa3 is ~30% above the crystallographic 150 Aa3 but soundly explained as solution thermal expansion (probe calibrated to recover 188 Aa3 on the static structure). Shortfall: key/abstract note increased helix-F flexibility vs WT; agent reports helix-F region RMSF 0.79 (below 0.91 protein mean, i.e. rigid) and ran no WT comparison, so this sub-claim is not reproduced. Two primary quantitative observables match; one qualitative sub-claim unconfirmed -> partial.
